role_title: Bilingual Staff Technical Program Manager, Global Enterprise Fulfillment Platform

location.office_time_required: Flex First — employees choose where they work; regular in-person events

secondary_archetype: Platform / Integration TPM with cross-border focus

level_signals: VP/C-suite partnership and executive visibility scope
Base pay range $202K–$255.5K depending on state, consistent with Staff-level IC
Mentors and onboards TPMs at varying levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of post-graduation experience in technology as a Technical Program Manager, Engineering Manager, Product Manager, or similar technical role
Fluent in Spanish (spoken and written) with daily collaboration experience across Spanish-speaking engineering, product, and commercial teams.
Proven track record leading end-to-end, company-wide programs of high complexity and ambiguity across multiple engineering organizations and cross-functional partners.
Demonstrated ability to create program structures and cross-program roadmaps; skilled at building execution plans and operating cadences tailored to program needs.
Deep technical domain knowledge enabling you to influence architectural decisions, de-risk complex technical choices, and guide implementation at org leadership level.
Exceptional communication skills, including building executive-level narratives and presenting to VP and C-suite audiences; adept at tailoring messages by audience and context.
Data-driven decision maker with a strong strategic mindset—able to define paths forward for ambiguous problems and identify new, high-impact problem areas.
Experience driving continuous improvement in processes, metrics, and program design; known for setting a high operational bar that others model.
Bachelor's degree in a technology or engineering discipline.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in a technology or engineering discipline.
Experience leading programs with active VP/C-suite engagement where outcomes directly influence company strategy and business results.
History of defining foundational, reusable program frameworks, tools, and processes adopted across large organizations.
Experience with AI/ML programs or demonstrated fluency applying AI to program management and engineering workflows.
Background in marketplace, e-commerce, or logistics technology, with familiarity in fulfillment, picking, delivery orchestration, or last-mile logistics.
Experience leading large-scale platform integration programs and coordinating distributed, multi-country engineering teams (e.g., across Latin America and Europe).

scope.team_size_estimate: TPM team of 12 within a 25-person broader TPM organization
scope.surface: Global Enterprise Fulfillment Platform — integrating Instaleap acquisition capabilities across North America, Latin America, Europe, and the Middle East
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS + marketplace platform serving enterprise retail partners
primary_metrics_inferred: Zero disruption to ~100 existing international retail partners during integration
Cross-sell conversion among existing partner base
Delivery alignment and integration milestone completion
Platform unification across fulfillment, picking, and delivery orchestration
core_responsibilities: Lead strategy, definition, and execution of company-wide programs with high ambiguity and executive visibility
Transform poorly understood problems into well-structured, cross-functional programs
Drive deep technical alignment across Engineering and Product on architectural decisions
Generate and maintain cross-program roadmaps coordinating multiple teams
Partner with VPs and executive leadership to align program goals to business strategy
Establish and evolve program frameworks adopted broadly across the organization
Mentor and onboard TPMs at varying levels
Lead integration of enterprise fulfillment, picking, delivery orchestration, and marketplace capabilities into a global platform
Serve as connective tissue between North America and Bogotá-based Instaleap teams
Ensure continuity for nearly 100 existing international retail partners during integration

compensation.equity_form: New hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range fully disclosed across multiple US state tiers ($202K–$255.5K base)
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Fully remote-eligible (#LI-Remote) with Flex First policy
Very specific, concrete scope (Instaleap acquisition integration, ~100 retail partners) — not vague
Clear team structure disclosed (12 TPMs in immediate team, 25-person broader org)
Mentorship culture and thought partnership explicitly described
red_flags: Hard bilingual Spanish requirement eliminates non-Spanish speakers entirely — highly specific filter
10+ years minimum is a strict floor with no stated equivalency exceptions
Cross-timezone coordination between US and Bogotá-based teams implies irregular working hours
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for non-US candidates
Scope is extremely broad (global platform + acquisition integration + ~100 partners) for a single TPM role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title, comp range, and scope description)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and enterprise retail partner context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as KPIs)
compensation.base_range_usd reported as full range min/max across all state tiers; CA/NY/CT/NJ high is $255,500, all-other-states low is $202,000
location.work_auth set to open (not stated; inferred from absence of restriction)
location.visa_sponsorship set to unclear (not mentioned in JD)
ambiguities: Spanish fluency appears twice in minimum qualifications (once as 'Fluency in Spanish' and once as 'Fluent in Spanish (spoken and written) with daily collaboration experience') — treated as one hard requirement with two entries to capture both phrasings
The role is tagged #LI-Remote and described as 'Flex First' with no city listed for this role specifically, but the company has offices; treated as remote-us-eligible
Compensation table covers US-based candidates only; international comp not stated despite global team collaboration scope
